name: list_posts
description: Lists posts from a Buffer organization with filters for channels, status, tags, and dates. Returns post details including id, status, via, text, scheduled/sent times, channel info, tags, assets, and errors. Supports pagination using Relay-style cursors. Set `includeMetrics: true` to additionally return per-post analytics on each post — only do so when the user asks about post performance, as it materially increases response size. For totals/sums across many posts, prefer `get_aggregated_post_metrics`. Call get_account first to obtain your organization ID, and list_channels to discover channel IDs.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization to list posts for. Use get_account to retrieve your organization IDs. Tell the user which organization (by name) you are querying.

channelIds.description: Filter posts by specific channel IDs. Use list_channels to discover available channel IDs.

items.description: A channel ID to filter posts by.

status.description: Filter posts by status (draft, needs_approval, scheduled, sending, sent, error)

items.description: A tag ID to filter posts by.

dueAt.description: Filter posts by their scheduled posting date

start.description: Include results with dates on or after this ISO 8601 date-time (e.g., "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z")

end.description: Include results with dates on or before this ISO 8601 date-time (e.g., "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z")

createdAt.description: Filter posts by the date they were created

start.description: Include results with dates on or after this ISO 8601 date-time (e.g., "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z")

end.description: Include results with dates on or before this ISO 8601 date-time (e.g., "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z")

field.description: The field to sort by (dueAt or createdAt)

first.description: Number of posts to return per page (default: 20, max: 100)

includeMetrics.description: Set true to also return per-post analytics (reactions, comments, impressions, etc.) and `metricsUpdatedAt` on each post. Default false — adds latency and response size; with up to 100 posts per page, the cost adds up. Only set true when the user asks about post performance. For totals across many posts, prefer `get_aggregated_post_metrics`.
